name: Compliance auditing & care plan QA
description: AI audits care plans against regulatory standards. Quik AI showed 42% compliance improvement.
current_process: Care home managers and compliance officers manually review care plans on a monthly or quarterly cycle, checking each plan against CQC quality statements, organisational policies, and best-practice guidelines. This involves reading through individual care plans, cross-referencing with daily logs, incident reports, and medication records, and checking that assessments are up to date, risk assessments are current, and person-centred outcomes are documented. Findings are recorded on paper audit templates or basic spreadsheets. Identified gaps require manual follow-up with individual staff members. Most care homes audit only a sample of care plans each month (e.g., 10–20%) due to time constraints, meaning issues in unaudited plans go undetected until the next cycle or a CQC inspection.
time_spent: Manual care plan auditing typically takes 30–60 minutes per care plan. A 60-bed care home auditing 20% of plans monthly reviews approximately 12 plans, consuming 6–12 hours of management time. Comprehensive quarterly audits of all plans can take 30–60 hours. Quik AI's Carey platform reports that managers save approximately 5 hours per week personally, while senior carers save around 1 hour per day. Across 12,007 real customer document audits (January–November 2025), the platform demonstrated these time savings at scale.
ai_solution: AI audits care plans continuously and at scale by analysing the full text of every care plan, daily log, and incident report against CQC quality statements, organisational standards, and best-practice guidelines. Specifically: (1) Automated document auditing — AI reads and scores every care plan against defined compliance criteria, not just a sample. (2) Gap identification — flags missing assessments, outdated risk reviews, incomplete person-centred outcomes, and inconsistencies between care plans and daily records. (3) Trend analysis — identifies patterns across residents (e.g., falling compliance in nutrition documentation) and across staff (e.g., specific shifts with lower documentation quality). (4) Remediation suggestions — provides best-practice care improvement recommendations and can rewrite documentation when standards change. (5) Continuous monitoring — replaces periodic auditing with real-time compliance scoring. Quik AI's Carey platform demonstrated a 42% jump in compliance across 12,007 audits, with compliance scores rising from 43% to 85% in the weakest care plans.
buyer_language: "I can only audit a fraction of our care plans each month — there just aren't enough hours. I know there are gaps in the ones I haven't checked, but I won't find them until CQC does. I need something that checks everything, all the time, without me having to read every word."
outcome_statement: Every care plan audited continuously against CQC standards — compliance scores improved by 42%, with gaps flagged and fixes suggested in real-time instead of discovered during inspections.
regulatory_alignment: Directly maps to CQC Quality Statements under Effective ('Assessing needs' — ensuring care plans are accurate and current), Safe (risk assessments up to date), and Well-Led (robust governance and quality assurance systems). Supports the CQC Single Assessment Framework's emphasis on continuous evidence gathering. Also aligns with the Digitising Social Care programme's goal of using digital tools to improve care quality and governance.
existing_tech_gap: Current care planning platforms (Nourish, Person Centred Software, Log my Care) store care plans digitally but do not analyse their content for compliance gaps. Audit tools like GoAudits and Care Audit Tool provide digital checklists but still require a human to read and assess each plan. Radar Healthcare offers quality management and incident tracking but not AI-powered care plan content analysis. AI adds unique value by reading and understanding the content of care plans at scale — something no current care platform does natively. Quik AI's Carey is the leading example, operating as a Chrome extension or API layer on top of existing care planning software (Nourish, Log my Care).
implementation_complexity: Plug-and-play to light integration. Quik AI's Carey operates as a Chrome extension that sits on top of existing care planning software, requiring minimal technical setup. Deeper integration via API (e.g., with Nourish or Log my Care) provides better automation but requires vendor cooperation. No process redesign is needed — AI auditing augments existing workflows rather than replacing them. Staff need brief training on interpreting AI-generated audit reports and acting on recommendations.
proof_of_concept_availability: Yes — strong proof of concept. Quik AI's Carey platform has published case studies with named UK care providers: Rose Care Group (compliance rising from 80% to 95%, managers saving 5 hours/week), Avante Care & Support (piloting with Nourish integration), and Wavemill (enhanced clinical reporting). The 42% compliance improvement figure is based on 12,007 real customer audits between January and November 2025. Quik AI has also formed a strategic partnership with Log my Care to expand access.
